name: Czechia
highlights: 1986: First box lacrosse game in Prague — players had handmade fibreglass sticks
1967: Neskenon Scout tournament — oldest European continental lacrosse event
NBLL: multi-country league (CZ, AT, DE, SK, PL), 28 games live-streamed 2024
Aleš Hřebeský Memorial 2026 (31st ed.): most prestigious European club box tournament
Pavel Dosly: 13th World Championship at age 44, 7 field + 6 box titles
note: Box lacrosse powerhouse. Historic depth since 1967. First field league 1992.

name: Germany
highlights: 2025: more German players in sixes than field + box combined
Die Finals 2025 (Dresden): first ever lacrosse on ARD public TV — 1M+ viewers
DLaxV admitted to DOSB (German Olympic Committee) Dec 2023 — LA28 pathway
Sixes 2025: Schwarz-Weiss Köln (men), HTHC Hamburg (women)
Field 2025: HTHC Hamburg (men), Schwarz-Weiss Köln (women)
HLC Rot-Weiß München: oldest club (1992), 10 titles in 10 years
note: Growth story of 2025. TV debut, sixes dominance, DOSB recognition, federal funding.

name: Sweden
highlights: Lacrosse introduced 1988 by American Jim Johnson; first championship 1992
Swedish Lacrosse League (SLL): sixes format, men & women + U21
Nordia Lacrosse Stockholm: 3× field champions (2015, 2018, 2025)
SNTDP: structured U16–U20 pathway; first U16 nationals 2018
Government economic support announced January 2025
note: Founding ELF member, solid SNTDP youth programme. Nordia is benchmark club.

name: Denmark
highlights: Founded 2002 by Kristian Gotsch after seeing lacrosse in Canada
Arc: growth (2003-09) → crisis (2010-15) → recovery (2014+) → 2025 Euros
Copenhagen Lacrosse Cup 2015: 9 men's + 5 women's international teams
Hired Nick Ravenhall (NZ 2014 World Games captain) in 2016 to rebuild
2025 Euros: France beat Denmark 14-4; goalkeeper David Gudman represented
note: Founded by one enthusiast. Commitment over dominance.

name: Poland
highlights: Host of 2025 European Men's Championship (Wrocław, Jul 9-19, 24 nations)
Also hosted 2022 EC + 2024 U20 EC — Wrocław is premier European venue
Awarded 2026-27 WL Men's & Women's Championship Division II (Wrocław)
Poznań Hussars (Dec 2007): first Polish lacrosse club
Poland Lacrosse Foundation: North American diaspora fundraising & recruitment
note: Infrastructure-focused growth. Hosting before dominance. Wrocław = world-class.

name: Switzerland
highlights: Founded 2006; World Lacrosse full member 2008
13 member clubs 2025-26, including new Lugano Falcons (Ticino)
Sixes championship final: Chur, Nov 8, 2025
Swiss national TV (RSI, Sep 2025) featured lacrosse as emerging sport
Pursuing recognition with Swiss Olympic Committee
note: Multi-format pioneer. Sixes data solid. Field/box history still needed.

name: France
highlights: AFL NOT yet officially recognised by CNOSF — working toward federation status
Paul Maître president since March 2024 (founder François Labbé 2008-2024)
Boxmania (since 2009): annual Western European box tournament — 10+ nations
She-Boxmania 2025: first women's edition; Team Edin-Bru won inaugural
Alps Sixes Open: May 23-24, Grenoble (mixed sixes, 2026 calendar)
Heritage team 2024: 50%+ French players live abroad — diaspora pool
note: No official federation status yet. Strengths: Boxmania & Olympic buzz.

name: Italy
highlights: 2025 EUROPEAN CHAMPIONSHIP SILVER MEDAL — first ever final appearance
Path: 4-0 pool → QF Czech Rep 18-9 → SF Ireland 10-8 → Final Israel 9-8
World ranking #9 (2025). Qualified for 2027 World Championship in Japan
Ryan Bell: 6G+4A vs Czech Republic | John Piatelli: 7 pts in QF
Under Federazione Italiana Hockey governance since 2023
note: 2025 breakthrough. Arc: underdog to European finalist.

name: England
highlights: 9 men's European Championship gold medals | 7 women's golds
2025: Women's EC CHAMPIONS + Men's Box EC CHAMPIONS
Men's 2025 SF: lost to Israel 8-6 (was defending champion)
Men's 2025 National Final: Spencer 13-10 Poynton
Greater Manchester hub: 18 of 22 U20 World Champ players from 17-mile radius
Pop Lacrosse programme reaches 1,000+ coaches outside UK
note: Historical dominant force. CEO: Mark Coups.

name: Ireland
highlights: Men's U20 European Championship 2024: GOLD (defeated England 10-7)
2025 EC: qualified for 2027 World Championship (QF win vs Germany)
Irish Lacrosse League (ILL) + Women's WILL + National Indoor NILL
Women's Box inaugural 2024 | Women's Sixes debut 2025 | Men's Sixes 2026
Heritage Cup 2025: Team Éire Green runners-up (32-team field)
note: Strong trajectory. U20 gold 2024 + senior 2027 qualifier. Full multi-format.

name: Netherlands
highlights: 2010 World Championship 8th place — best field result ever
DNLL (men): 12 teams | WDNLL (women): 6 teams
NLL pros: Sam Haines (Toronto Rock) | Joshua Toguri (Rochester Knighthawks)
Neal Powless: 9× World Champ, former Haudenosaunee player, coach since 2017
2025 Euros: qualified for 2027 World Championship Division II
note: 12-team domestic league. Two NLL professionals. Consistent international.

name: Finland
highlights: Hosted inaugural European Box Lacrosse Championship 2017 (Turku, 14 nations)
2025 EC pool: Finland 19 - Bulgaria 0
Qualified for 2027 World Championship Division II
Praha26 box project: European Box Champ Jun 24-Jul 4, 2026 Prague
Tryouts Sep 26-28 2025 (Turku); monthly training from Oct 2025
note: EBLC 2017 host. Growing box programme targeting Prague 2026.

name: Belgium
highlights: ELF Easter Tournament (Westerlo): flagship annual event (Apr 2024)
Ken Galluccio Cup: ELF official club championship, held annually 5+ years
12 active clubs across Flanders & Wallonia
2025 European Championship participation confirmed
note: Easter Tournament host. 12 active clubs. Benelux regional hub.

name: Croatia
highlights: Lacrosse founded 2011 Zagreb; first national team 2016
Dalmatia Lacrosse Cup (Split): since 2021, 24+ teams from 9 countries
2025 EC: qualified for 2027 World Championship Division II
Policy: 85% passport holders minimum, 15% heritage max
note: Adriatic destination tournament. Young program, strong 2025 result.

name: Hungary
highlights: Budapest Blax Lacrosse: founded March 2008 via NLL TV broadcasts
2009: entered Austrian Lacrosse League — first international competition
Hosted 2016 European Championships (Gödöllő, 24+ nations)
Heritage Cup 2025: Men's Open 20th (32-team field)
note: Founded by one enthusiast via TV. EC 2016 host.

name: Spain
highlights: Liga Española de Lacrosse: 9th edition+, 8 men's clubs
2025 EC: Spain 16-4 Sweden; qualified for 2027 World Champ Division II
HOST of 2026 European Sixes Championship (Salou): Nov 2-18
2026 ESC is 2027 World Lacrosse Sixes qualifier — Olympic pathway
note: 2026 European Sixes host — key Olympic pathway event.

name: Scotland
highlights: Origin: Lockerbie disaster 1988 — Syracuse challenged Scotland to form national team
Annual Scotland vs Syracuse memorial match in Lockerbie
2025 Home Internationals host (Apr 4-6) — 16 squads, 6 nations
Women's 2024 EC (Braga): bronze medal, ranked #10 globally
note: Unique founding story (Lockerbie). Strong university system.

name: Wales
highlights: Women's medal streak: every EC since 1996 (except 2003)
Women's 2026 World Championship: qualified
Heritage Cup 2025 Women's: runners-up (Argentina 8-4 Wales)
note: Women's programme excellence since 1996.

name: Norway
highlights: ~1,000 registered players, 16 active clubs nationwide
NHH Vikings (Bergen, 2004) + BI-Lions (Oslo, 2005): founding university clubs
Oslo Legends: 8× national champions
2025 EC: narrow win vs Portugal; qualified for 2027 Div II
note: University-based model. 16 clubs, solid domestic league.

name: Slovakia
highlights: Bratislava Tricksters (2003): 9× Slovak Lacrosse League champions
2022 European Box Champ (Hannover): 5th place — historic best
Canadian Lacrosse League partnership 2023: heritage recruitment + coaching
2025 EC: dramatic comeback vs Greece; qualified for 2027 Div II
note: CLL partnership is unique asset. Central European box hub.

name: Slovenia
highlights: Lacrosse Olimpija & LK Ljubljana: both founded 2018
LK Ljubljana: 2× State Champion (2023)
First youth lacrosse programme in Slovenia at LK Ljubljana
Donau Lacrosse League: regional competition (AT, HU, surrounds)
note: 2 active clubs, both Ljubljana. Youth outreach focus.

name: Israel
highlights: 2025 EUROPEAN CHAMPIONSHIP GOLD — defeated Italy 9-8 (Jul 19, Wrocław)
Path: 4-0 pool → QF Czech Rep 8-7 → SF England 8-6 → Final Italy 9-8
World ranking #7 globally, #1 Europe (2025)
Daniel Kraft Family National Lacrosse Center (Netanya): only dedicated facility outside North America
Box: #5 world — won inaugural EBLC 2017 (defeated Czech 8-7)
Women's: #6 world, #2 Europe
note: 2025 European Champions. Diaspora + grassroots model.

name: Greece
highlights: Founded July 2014 during 2014 World Championship (Denver)
President: Anthony Katagas (Oscar-winning producer, '12 Years a Slave')
Boots on the Ground (2017+): 400+ students, Anatolia College Thessaloniki
YMCA Lacrosse Academy: first dedicated lacrosse academy in Greece
Greece Lacrosse Sixes Tourney: 3rd annual Oct 18-19, 2025 (Thessaloniki)
note: Oscar-winner as president. Diaspora + grassroots model.

name: Portugal
highlights: Lisboa Gladiators: first club Sep 2015
EuroLax Sixes Cup (Monte Gordo, Algarve): annual flagship, 350+ players
2025 Women's Sixes Qualifier (Mar 4-9): 12 teams for World Games qualification
Lisboa Lacrosse Cup: 9th edition 2025, 16 teams from 6+ countries
Women's national team debut 2024
note: EuroLax Sixes Cup host. Rapid expansion post-2022.

name: Austria
highlights: Vienna Monarchs (2007): flagship club, 20th anniversary 2025
NBLL 2025: first Austrian NBLL team — historic inaugural participation
Twin Stick Bavaria: partnership with VER Selb ice hockey for summer training
Multi-league model: Czech NBLL + Slovak SLL + Donau regional
note: Vienna Monarchs driving box growth. Cross-border model.

name: Turkey
highlights: Founded 2009 by Patrick Dougherty — 'Father of Turkish Lacrosse'
First predominantly Muslim-majority nation in FIL + ELF
2014 World Championship debut (22nd place)
Women's national team formation underway 2024-2025
Balkans League planning with Bulgaria, Serbia
note: Historic milestone: first Muslim-majority nation in international lacrosse.

name: Bulgaria
highlights: Founded 2007 by Nikolay Cholakov
Bulgarian Olympic Committee recognition 2024-2025
Head coach Tucker LaBelle (2024): homegrown talent only — no heritage players
2025 EC pool: Finland 19 - Bulgaria 0 (group stage)
Balkans League development with Serbia, Turkey, Croatia
note: Pure homegrown model. Olympic recognition 2024-25.

name: Estonia
highlights: ELF member
Baltic Council of Ministers Presidency 2026
Nordic-Baltic Eight (NB8) cooperation
2025 EC participation (Wrocław) confirmed
note: Baltic regional coordination role.

name: Latvia
highlights: ELF member
Baltic Council of Ministers Presidency 2025 (Riga)
NB8 Coordinator
2025 EC participation confirmed
note: Baltic regional hub.

name: Lithuania
highlights: ELF member
Baltic Council of Ministers Presidency 2027
NB8 leadership 2023-2024
2025 EC participation confirmed
note: Third Baltic state. BCM 2027 presidency.

name: Luxembourg
highlights: ELF member | Population ~630K
Cross-border model: players compete in Belgian DNLL + French regional
Ken Galluccio Cup participation
2025 EC participation confirmed
note: Micro-nation cross-border model.

name: Malta
highlights: ELF member | Island nation, population ~535K
Youth-first strategy: school integration priority
PE class integration across Maltese education
Summer youth camps (ages 6-18)
note: Island nation. Youth-first model. Mediterranean expansion potential.

name: Serbia
highlights: ELF member
NBLL box lacrosse participation
Belgrade-based primary clubs
Balkans League coordination with Bulgaria, Turkey, Croatia
note: Central European box hub. Balkans growth catalyst.

name: Armenia
highlights: ELF member | Diaspora-driven model
Armenian-American diaspora from Boston primary player source
Yerevan-based headquarters
Heritage Cup (Boston) flagship participation
note: Diaspora-first model. Boston Armenian community drives programme.

name: Ukraine
highlights: War impact Feb 2022+: training suspended, players displaced across Europe
2025 recovery: national team rebuilding, youth programme restarting
UNDP adaptive sports training Mar 2025 Kyiv: lacrosse for war-affected youth
Athletes for Ukraine coalition: solidarity & peace platform
2025 EC: both national teams competing in Wrocław
note: Resilience narrative. Sport as nation-building during recovery.
